claim: Plain gallows characters EVA 't' and 'p' are non-phonetic paragraph/section markers, not consonants: 'p' shows 5.4x line-initial enrichment (70.9% vs 13.1% baseline), 't' shows 3.2x (42.3%), while bench gallows (cth/ckh/cph) appear in normal mid-word positions.
evidence_for: Direct positional statistics computable from corpus
Stripping plain gallows as prefixes recovers 4.3pp coverage in Brady's pipeline
Consistent with other researchers who've noted gallows positional anomalies
evidence_against: No known paleographic analogue for mid-word phonetic vs. line-initial marker distinction within a single script
test: Compute line-initial frequency of EVA 't','p','cth','ckh','cph' against 13.1% baseline
test_threshold: 'p' >=4.0x and 't' >=2.5x, bench gallows <1.5x: confirmed. Otherwise: challenged.
tests_remaining: Compute line-initial frequency of EVA 't','p','cth','ckh','cph' against 13.1% baseline
reasoning: Testable today from existing corpus. Independent of Syriac hypothesis.
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
